data.title: Kelun-Biotech's Trastuzumab Botidotin approved by NMPA for HER2-positive BC treatment.
data.description: A biopharmaceutical company based in Chengdu, China, recently revealed that their human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 (HER2)-directed antibody-drug conjugate (ADC) trastuzumab botidotin, also referred to as A166, has received approval. This news was disclosed on October 17, 2025.
BoE's Pill sees rate cuts if the economy evolves as forecast
BoE's Pill: There is a risk that self-sustaining inflationary dynamics embed in expectations

data.title: PPI inflation rises, dampening hopes for big Fed rate cut; S&P 500 drops. Trump admin wants half-point cut next month.
data.description: The Trump administration is advocating for a 0.5% reduction in interest rates next month. However, hopes for a significant Federal Reserve rate cut were dampened as hot Producer Price Index inflation data was released. This led to a decline in the S&P 500 index.

data.title: Core CPI inflation tops 3%, won't stop Fed rate cuts. S&P 500 rises despite the increase.
data.description: The latest data shows that the core Consumer Price Index (CPI) inflation rate has begun to increase once again, surpassing the 3% mark. Despite this uptick, experts believe that the rise is not significant enough to deter the Federal Reserve from implementing further interest rate cuts. This news led to a positive response in the stock market, with the S&P 500 index experiencing growth following the announcement.

data.title: Inflation rate exceeds Fed target, layoffs spike by 140% yoy in July; S&P 500 rises. Fed's preferred inflation metric surged.
data.description: The latest data reveals a significant increase of 140% in job reductions compared to the previous year during the month of July. The Federal Reserve's preferred measure of inflation exceeded expectations. Concurrently, the S&P 500 index saw a rise in value.

data.title: Fed may consider rate cut after EU tariff deal eases inflation fears post Trump's trade deals.
data.description: The Federal Reserve could potentially have reduced concerns about inflation caused by tariffs following the recent trade agreements secured by President Trump. This development may signal a potential openness to a reduction in interest rates by the Fed.

data.title: CPI in June indicates inflation rise due to Trump tariffs, affecting S&P 500.
data.description: In June, the consumer price index revealed a rise in inflation due to the Trump tariffs, indicating their impact on the economy. The S&P 500 also experienced a decrease in value as a result.

data.title: UCLOUDLINK partners with Aham Al-Tafasil to bring PetPhone to Middle East, receiving 30,000-unit order from Saudi Arabian-based distributor MASO.
data.description: On October 17, 2025, UCLOUDLINK GROUP INC. revealed that its innovative product, PetPhone, the world's first smartphone for pets, secured a significant order of 30,000 units from Aham Al-Tafasil Trading Co. (MASO), a prominent distributor in Saudi Arabia catering to pet supplies and consumer electronics. This groundbreaking device aims to revolutionize pet care through features like real-time communication, advanced tracking, health monitoring, and a dedicated community for pet enthusiasts. UCLOUDLINK, recognized as the primary mobile data traffic sharing marketplace globally, operates under the NASDAQ symbol UCL.

data.title: ADP jobs report and jobless claims show slow hiring; backing Fed rate-cut bets. Labor market indicators soft.
data.description: The latest ADP jobs report, as well as initial jobless claims, contribute to a series of weak labor market signals, fueling expectations for quick action from the Federal Reserve to cut rates. The ADP report indicated modest hiring, while jobless claims increased, supporting speculation of a Fed rate cut.

data.title: Trump's pressure may have influenced the Fed to cut rates, says Steve Liesman, as political factors shape monetary policy.
data.description: According to Senior Economics Reporter Steve Liesman of CNBC, political pressure from President Donald Trump might have influenced the recent decision of the Federal Reserve to cut interest rates. Liesman mentioned that the Federal Reserve operates within a political context and used the example of its aggressive stance against inflation in 2022 to illustrate the impact of political dynamics on monetary policy. During that time, officials were granted political approval to combat inflation vigorously. Liesman further explained that this permission is reflected in a holistic manner. Additionally, another Federal Reserve official, Michael Barr, has warned that inflation could persist until 2027 and suggested that interest rates may continue to remain high for an extended period. The complete article can be found on Benzinga.com.
